claim: PingSkills is run by Jeff Plumb (Sydney 2000 Olympian) and Alois Rosario, who has coached the Australian Olympic team and Paralympic teams from 2012 through 2024.
quote: Jeff Plumb won the qualification tournament for the Sydney 2000 Olympics... Alois Rosario's coaching journey has included guiding National Junior champions and Olympic athletes... at the 2012 London Paralympics, the 2016 Rio Paralympics, the 2020 Tokyo Paralympics, and the 2024 France Paralympics.

claim: PingSkills YouTube has approximately 200K subscribers, having earned the YouTube Silver Play Button.
quote: PingSkills - Table Tennis Tutorials 200K subs on YouTube

claim: PingSkills publishes dedicated serve tutorials including a step-by-step Backspin Reverse Pendulum Serve breakdown.
quote: Learn the Backspin Reverse Pendulum Serve | Table Tennis | PingSkills

claim: Tom Lodziak is a Table Tennis England Level 2 coach based in Cambridge who has trained under Richard Prause (Timo Boll's former coach), Desmond Douglas and others; his YouTube has over 275,000 subscribers and 50 million views.
quote: Tom Lodziak is a Table Tennis England Level 2 coach based in Cambridge, England... received coaching from many top players and coaches including Richard Prause (Timo Boll's former coach), Rade Markovic, Desmond Douglas... popular YouTube channel with over 275,000 subscribers and 50 million video views.

claim: Tom Lodziak's serve content explicitly targets amateur and league players, with detailed pendulum/sidespin serve walkthroughs that distinguish sidespin-backspin and sidespin-topspin variations.
quote: Epic sidespin serve tutorial... how to do a long fast sidespin serve, also known as a pendulum serve. The serve has two variations – sidespin with backspin and sidespin with topspin

claim: TableTennisDaily founder Dan Ives is a former semi-professional player in France with a first-class honours degree in Sports Coaching & Performance and 15+ years of coaching experience.
quote: Dan Ives, with 15+ years of coaching experience, is the founder of TableTennisDaily... set up TableTennisDaily in 2010 when he was playing semi-professional table tennis in France... first-class honours' degree in Sports coaching and Performance

claim: TableTennisDaily Academy collaborator Andrew Baggaley is a multiple English national champion who reached a world ranking of No. 139 and was England No. 1.
quote: A multiple English national champion, Andrew Baggaley turned professional aged 13... Highest World Ranking of No. 139 and Highest England Ranking of No. 1

claim: PingSunday is run by Coach EmRatThich, a Vietnamese-born, France-based club coach (PhD from Sorbonne) who teaches via the Chinese coaching philosophy with a 250K-subscriber YouTube following.
quote: Coach EmRatThich was born in Hanoi, Vietnam in 1983... After finishing his Ph.D. in Paris, University Pierre Marie Curie (Sorbonne University) in 2011, he is now a table tennis coach in a small club in France... Using the Chinese coaching philosophy

claim: Adam Bobrow is the official ITTF/World Table Tennis commentator since 2014 and his main YouTube channel had ~1.43M subscribers and 429M views as of late 2025; his content skews entertainment with some technique demonstrations.
quote: In 2014, Bobrow won a competition to become the 'voice of table tennis', and has been the official commentator for the International Table Tennis Federation ever since... approximately 1.43 million subscribers with 429 million views

claim: Pongfinity is a Finnish entertainment-leaning trio (Emil, Otto, Miikka) with 4.67M YouTube subscribers — the largest table-tennis channel — though Otto and Miikka are semi-pro Finnish national team players, not coaches.
quote: Pongfinity has 4.67 million subscribers, and their YouTube channel is the 7th most-subscribed channel in Finland and the most-subscribed channel about table tennis... Both Otto and Miikka are semi-professional players, and both represented Finland during the European and World Table Tennis Championships

claim: Coach Brian Pace of Dynamic Table Tennis is a USATT Certified National Coach with a peak USATT rating of 2610, top-300 world ranking, three collegiate championships, and a 4-0 record coaching students to national titles.
quote: Brian is a USATT National Level Coach... 3-Time Collegiate Champions... peak USATT rating of 2610 and was ranked within the top 300 in the world... coached his students to National Titles with a record of 4-0

claim: ExpertTableTennis is run by Ben Larcombe, a UKCC Level 2 coach (~150th in England) best known for the viral 'Expert in a Year' project; his channel skews podcast/long-form analysis over high-frequency video tutorials.
quote: Ben Larcombe... is a professional table tennis coach. He started Expert Table Tennis back in 2012... UKCC level 2 table tennis coach... 'Expert in a Year' challenge... almost 3 million views

claim: Independent reviews place PingSkills, TableTennisDaily, Tom Lodziak and PingSunday/EmRatThich at the top of recommended coaching channels, with Pongfinity and Adam Bobrow filling the entertainment tier; PechPongTT (Seth Pech, USA national team) is rising as a tactical-analysis option.
quote: PingSkills | Beginner-focused technique instruction; straightforward explanations | Jeff Plumb (Sydney 2000 Olympian); Alois Rosario (World Championships)... PechPongTT | In-depth tactical and technical tutorials... Seth Pech - semi-professional American player; USA national team experience
